content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Adjusted backend target resolution so the new compiled Codex defaults remain in place for implementers and final reviewers, but explicit top-level `default_backend`/`default_model` overrides now win over those compiled role-model defaults when they apply to the resolved family. Updated diagnostics to report the same precedence order, and added regressions covering implementer/final-review `show-effective` and `probe` behavior for the restored override path.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Inspect the backend policy and diagnostics resolution order to confirm that merged compiled `backends.codex.role_models.*` defaults were incorrectly outranking explicit `default_model` overrides for implementer and final-review targets.
2. [Completed] Patch `src/contexts/agent_execution/policy.rs` so explicit workspace/project role-model overrides still win first, explicit base-backend/default-model overrides win next for the matching family, and compiled role defaults only apply as fallback after those explicit settings.
3. [Completed] Patch diagnostics source attribution to mirror the new precedence order, add regression coverage for policy resolution plus `show-effective`/`probe`, and rerun the required validation suite.

## Validation Evidence

- `cargo test` passed.
- `cargo clippy` passed.
- `cargo fmt --check` passed.
- New policy coverage now asserts that `default_backend = "codex"` plus `default_model = "workspace-default-model"` resolves the default implementer and first final-review reviewer to `workspace-default-model` instead of the compiled Codex role defaults.
- New diagnostics coverage now asserts that `show-effective` reports `workspace.toml` as the model source for implementer and final-review Codex defaults when an explicit top-level default model overrides them, and that `backend probe --role implementer` / `final_reviewer` resolve the same overridden model.

## Outstanding Risks

- The worktree still contains pre-existing unrelated modifications in `.ralph-burning/projects/utm/journal.ndjson` and `.ralph-burning/projects/utm/run.json`, which I left untouched.
- `cargo clippy` still reports existing warning-level findings outside this change set (for example `src/adapters/fs.rs:1122`), but the command completed successfully.
